title: Helianthemum piloselloides
taxonomy: Helianthemum piloselloides is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Cistaceae. It belongs to the genus Helianthemum, a group of flowering plants commonly known as rockrose. The genus Helianthemum is further classified under the order Malvales and the class Magnoliopsida. Within the kingdom Plantae, Helianthemum piloselloides is a member of the phylum Magnoliophyta. This plant's classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other flowering plants.
uses: Helianthemum piloselloides, also known as rockrose, has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments. The plant's leaves and flowers contain compounds with potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. In traditional medicine, it has been used to treat skin conditions, such as wounds, eczema, and acne. Some studies suggest that rockrose extracts may have antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of Helianthemum piloselloides for medicinal purposes.
benefits: Helianthemum piloselloides has been found to have various health benefits, primarily due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its potential health benefits. Research suggests that it may help protect against cell damage, reduce inflammation, and even exhibit antimicrobial activities. Additionally, Helianthemum piloselloides has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory infections. More studies are needed to fully understand the plant's health benefits and potential applications.
properties: Helianthemum piloselloides, also known as rockrose, is valued for its therapeutic properties, particularly in traditional medicine. constituents: Helianthemum piloselloides contains a range of active constituents, including flavonoids and phenolic acids. These compounds contribute to the plant's potential medicinal properties and antioxidant effects. parts: Helianthemum piloselloides is a species of flowering plant used in herbal medicine. The leaves and flowers of Helianthemum piloselloides are considered the medicinal parts, often used to treat various health issues. Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat digestive problems, such as diarrhea and indigestion, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The leaves and flowers of Helianthemum piloselloides are often made into teas, infusions, or used in tinctures to utilize their medicinal properties. However, it is essential to consult a healthcare professional before using the plant for medicinal purposes.
preparations: Helianthemum piloselloides is often used in herbal preparations, particularly in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, where its leaves and flowers are dried and used for medicinal purposes. The plant is believed to possess anti-inflammatory properties, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. side_effects: Helianthemum piloselloides, also known as rockrose, is generally considered safe for use in herbal remedies. However, it may cause skin irritation or allergic reactions in some individuals. There is limited information available on the long-term effects of consuming Helianthemum piloselloides. It's also unknown if it interacts with any medications. More research is needed to fully understand the potential side effects of using Helianthemum piloselloides.
